description:
You are acting as a platform engineer who has just finished modifying a set of Kubernetes manifests that live in
    /home/user/k8s/manifests.  
    The directory already exists and (for the purposes of this exercise) currently contains exactly three YAML files:

        /home/user/k8s/manifests/deployment.yaml
        /home/user/k8s/manifests/service.yaml
        /home/user/k8s/manifests/configmap.yaml

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create a gzip-compressed tar archive of the entire /home/user/k8s/manifests directory.  
       • The archive *must* be created at the absolute path /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz.  
       • The resulting tarball must include the manifests directory itself as the top-level item, so that when someone
         runs “tar -tzf /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz” the very first path listed is
         “k8s/manifests/”.  
    2. Immediately after creating the archive, append a single log line containing only the absolute path to the archive
       (i.e. “/home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz”) to the file /home/user/backup.log.  
       • If /home/user/backup.log does not already exist, your command must create it automatically.  
       • Do **not** add extra spaces, newline characters, timestamps or anything else—just the full path followed by the
         default single trailing newline that echo normally produces.  
    3. Achieve both actions (archiving + logging) with one compound shell invocation.  
       • You may use command chaining such as “&&” or “;” so long as everything runs as a *single* line entered in the
         terminal.

    Output format requirements that automated tests will verify  
    -----------------------------------------------------------  
    1. The file /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz must exist.  
    2. Running “tar -tzf /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz” must list exactly these three entries (order does not
       matter, but the paths are case-sensitive):  
           k8s/manifests/deployment.yaml  
           k8s/manifests/service.yaml  
           k8s/manifests/configmap.yaml  
    3. The file /home/user/backup.log must exist and contain *exactly one* non-empty line:  

           /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz  

       (including the trailing newline). No other characters are allowed.

    Hint  
    ----  
    A typical solution could combine “tar” and “echo” (or printf) in a single chained command; however, do *not* copy
    commands from this hint verbatim.  
    Remember: you only need **one** terminal command line to accomplish both steps.
truth:
Initial filesystem state  
    ------------------------  
    1. Directory /home/user/k8s/manifests/ exists.  
    2. Files and their exact contents:

       /home/user/k8s/manifests/deployment.yaml  
       -----------------------------------------
       apiVersion: apps/v1
       kind: Deployment
       metadata:
         name: demo-deployment
       spec:
         replicas: 2
         selector:
           matchLabels:
             app: demo
         template:
           metadata:
             labels:
               app: demo
           spec:
             containers:
             - name: demo
               image: nginx:1.21

       /home/user/k8s/manifests/service.yaml  
       --------------------------------------
       apiVersion: v1
       kind: Service
       metadata:
         name: demo-service
       spec:
         selector:
           app: demo
         ports:
         - protocol: TCP
           port: 80
           targetPort: 80
         type: ClusterIP

       /home/user/k8s/manifests/configmap.yaml  
       ----------------------------------------
       apiVersion: v1
       kind: ConfigMap
       metadata:
         name: demo-config
       data:
         example.property.1: hello
         example.property.2: world

    3. The file /home/user/backup.log does *not* exist at the start.

    Desired post-task filesystem state  
    ----------------------------------  
    1. /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.  
       Listing its contents with “tar -tzf /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz” must produce paths (in any order):

           k8s/manifests/deployment.yaml
           k8s/manifests/service.yaml
           k8s/manifests/configmap.yaml

       Note: no leading “./” in the stored paths.

    2. /home/user/backup.log exists and contains exactly:

           /home/user/manifests_backup.tar.gz\n     (single newline at the end)

    The automated checker will confirm that both files exist, that the tarball contains *all three* YAML manifests under
    the “k8s/manifests/” prefix, and that backup.log has precisely one line with the absolute path above.
